chapter_context.summary: Addresses a severe drought in the land, records Jeremiah's passionate intercession, and details God's rejection of prayers due to persistent false prophets.

outline.text: Severe Drought in Judah: Cry of Jerusalem and Fainting Cattle
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Severe Drought in Judah: Cry of Jerusalem and Fainting Cattle (14:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Jeremiah's Intercession: 'O the hope of Israel, why as a stranger?'
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jeremiah's Intercession: 'O the hope of Israel, why as a stranger?' (14:7-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Rebuking False Prophets; Sword and Famine Proclaimed
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuking False Prophets; Sword and Famine Proclaimed (14:13-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Heartfelt Appeal: 'Do not abhor us, for thy name's sake'
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Heartfelt Appeal: 'Do not abhor us, for thy name's sake' (14:19-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Warning
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of warning, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 14, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
